original_image_tags: A typed letter on aged paper, torn at the edges and showing signs of wear. The text is partially obscured by stamps.

elements.description: A scan of a typed document, appearing as a mirror image or bleed-through from the reverse side of the paper. The text includes a letterhead from the Department of Justice, Federal Bureau of Investigation, Washington, D.C., addressed to Mr. Harry H. Bond, dated May 28, 1950. There are handwritten annotations, rubber stamps (including 'RECEIVED a Type', 'RECEIVED - 3E', 'INDEXED - 3E', and a red stamp), and a large blue ink signature or scribble near the bottom.

judge.reason: Description A describes the document as a typed letter on aged, torn paper with obscured text, while Description B provides specific, detailed content (Department of Justice letterhead, date, addressee, multiple specific stamp texts, signature) that contradicts A's general description and implies A missed all specific visual content.
